title: Count failed logins
prompt: Write count_failed_logins(lines) — a list of log lines — returning how many contain the phrase 'Failed password'.

tests.call: count_failed_logins(['Failed password for root','Accepted password for admin','Failed password for guest'])

help.plain: Count how many log lines are failed-login attempts.
help.analogy: Like tallying rejected keycard swipes in a building's access log.
help.mnemonic: Count the lines that say 'Failed password' — not just 'password' (that catches successes too).
help.nudge: A successful login also contains the word 'password'. Match the full phrase 'Failed password' to avoid counting those.

details.restate: Given lines from an auth log, count only the failed login attempts — the ones containing 'Failed password'. Note: successful logins say 'Accepted password', so don't match on 'password' alone.

io.call: count_failed_logins(['Failed password for root','Accepted password for admin'])
io.because: only the first line is a failure; the second is a success

terms.meaning: a system log of login attempts, successful and failed
